author: y4rr
content:
Result: meta device now propagates through arithmetic, reduction, linalg, and activation ops, enabling dry-run model construction without allocation.

**New module: ferrotorch-core/src/meta_propagate.rs**

Helper module providing four propagation primitives that ops call at the top of their implementations:

- `unary_same_shape(input)` — for elementwise unary ops that return same shape (relu, sqrt, neg, abs, etc.). Returns Some(meta_out) when input is meta, None otherwise so the caller continues to its normal path.
- `binary_broadcast(a, b)` — for elementwise binary ops with NumPy broadcasting (add, sub, mul, div). Returns Some(broadcast_meta) when both inputs are meta. Errors when only one is meta — there's no defined behavior for mixing real and meta tensors since the real side has data the meta side does not.
- `reduce_dim(input, dim, keepdim)` — for sum_dim/mean_dim/etc. Handles negative axis indexing, errors on out-of-bounds dim. Output shape removes (or replaces with 1) the reduced axis.
- `reduce_all(input)` — for sum/mean/prod that collapse to a scalar. Output is shape [].
- `matmul(a, b)` — full PyTorch matmul shape rules: 1D×1D dot, 2D×1D mv, 1D×2D vm, 2D×2D mm, batched broadcast for ≥3D.

All five helpers detect mixed meta/real inputs and return DeviceMismatch.

**Instrumented ops** (each starts with a meta check that early-returns the meta result):

Arithmetic (grad_fns/arithmetic.rs): add, sub, mul, div (binary broadcast), neg, sqrt, abs, pow (unary).

Reductions (grad_fns/reduction.rs): sum, mean, prod (full reductions to scalar), sum_dim, mean_dim (single-axis reductions).

Linalg (ops/linalg.rs): matmul (the public top-level entry point that branches to dot/mv/vm/mm/broadcast_matmul; the meta check intercepts before that branch).

Activations (grad_fns/activation.rs): relu, sigmoid, tanh, gelu_with (so gelu inherits via delegation), silu, softmax.

The meta checks all sit BEFORE the profiler scope wrappers and BEFORE any device dispatch, so meta tensors take a fast path with zero overhead beyond the is_meta() Option<bool> check.

**Tests added (28 in meta_propagate.rs):**

22 unit tests for the helpers themselves: shape propagation, mixed-meta error path, broadcast rules, negative axis indexing, all 6 matmul shape cases (dot/mv/vm/mm/batched/batched-broadcast), out-of-bounds errors, CPU pass-through.

6 end-to-end tests calling the actual instrumented public ops:
- `test_e2e_meta_arithmetic_chain` — add → mul → neg → sqrt all stay meta
- `test_e2e_meta_arithmetic_with_broadcast` — [5,1,7] add [3,1] → [5,3,7]
- `test_e2e_meta_reductions` — sum to scalar, sum_dim, mean_dim with keepdim
- `test_e2e_meta_matmul` — [8,16] @ [16,32] → [8,32]
- `test_e2e_meta_activations` — all 6 activations preserve shape
- **`test_e2e_meta_mlp_dry_run`** — the canonical use case: simulate a 2-layer MLP forward (matmul → add → relu → matmul → add) on meta inputs, verify the output shape is [16, 10] without any tensor allocation. This proves you can dry-run model construction at scale.

925 ferrotorch-core lib tests pass (was 897, +28). `cargo build --workspace` clean with no new warnings.

**Scope:** Arithmetic, reduction, linalg-matmul, and the six core activations are instrumented. Conv1d/2d/3d, normalization (BatchNorm/LayerNorm/etc.), embedding, attention, and shape-only ops (reshape/view/permute — most already work via storage but should get explicit checks) are NOT yet instrumented and still error on meta inputs. Each follows the same one-line pattern (`if let Some(out) = meta_propagate::xxx(...)? { return Ok(out); }`); rolling them out is mechanical follow-up work.
